title: Parahebe trifida
intro: Parahebe trifida, also known as the New Zealand parahebe or mountain parahebe, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, and may aid in the treatment of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Parahebe trifida include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects.
taxonomy: Parahebe trifida is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The species is classified in the genus Parahebe, which is a group of small to medium-sized plants. Parahebe trifida is often found in open areas, such as grasslands and rocky outcrops. The plant is characterized by its small white or pink flowers and trifoliate leaves.
